subject: Chemistry
q: The total change in the oxidation state of manganese involved in the reaction of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KMnO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and potassium iodide in the acidic medium is ________.
question: The total change in the oxidation state of manganese involved in the reaction of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>KMnO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math> and potassium iodide in the acidic medium is ________.
solution:
In the reaction of KMnO4 and potassium iodide (KI) in an acidic medium, the KMnO4  acts as an oxidizing agent, and the iodide ions (I-) in KI act as reducing agents.   

KMnO4+KI+H+⟶Mn+2+I2+H2O

In KMnO4, the oxidation state of manganese is +7, which is the highest possible oxidation state for manganese in this compound.

In the final product, MnSO4, the oxidation state of manganese is +2.

Therefore, the total change in the oxidation state of manganese is:

+7 (initial) - +2 (final) = 5

Hence the change in O.S. of Mn is 5.

subject: Chemistry
q:
2MnO4-+bC2O42-+cH+→xMn2++yCO2+zH2O
If the above equation is balanced with integer coefficients, the value of c is _______ . (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
question:
2MnO4-+bC2O42-+cH+→xMn2++yCO2+zH2O
If the above equation is balanced with integer coefficients, the value of c is _______ . (Round off to the Nearest Integer).
solution:
Writing the half reaction

Reduction half reaction 

MnO4-→Mn2+

balancing oxygen 

MnO4-→Mn2++4H2O

balancing Hydrogen 

8H++MnO4-→Mn2++4H2O

balancing charge 

5e-+8H++MnO4-→Mn2++4H2O

Oxidation half 

C2O42-→CO2
Balancing carbon

C2O42-→2CO2

Balancing charge

C2O42-→2CO2+2e-

Net equation

16H++2MnO4-+5C2O42-→10CO2+2Mn2++8H2O

So c=16

subject: Chemistry
q: In which one of the following sets all species show disproportionation reaction?
question: In which one of the following sets all species show disproportionation reaction?
solution:
Disproportionation reactions are a special type of redox reactions. One of the reactants in a disproportionation reaction always contains an element that can exist in at least three oxidation states. The element of reacting species is in intermediate oxidation state and simultaneously gets oxidised and reduced. MnO42-,ClO2-,Cl2 and Mn3+ are having intermediate oxidation states.

Note: No option is correct in the actual question. One option is modified for its correctness.

subject: Chemistry
q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msubsup><mi>IO</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>xI</mi><mo>-</mo></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>12</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>+</mo></msup><mo>→</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>   What is the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math>?
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msubsup><mi>IO</mi><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>xI</mi><mo>-</mo></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>12</mn><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>+</mo></msup><mo>→</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>   What is the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></math>?
solution:
Assign the oxidation number to all the elements in the reaction,

2I+5O3-+xI--1+12H++1→6I20+6H2O

2IO3-+10e-→I2

2I-→I2+2e-×5

2IO3-+10I-+12H+→6I2+6H2O

Therefore, the value of  x=10 in the reaction.

q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.5</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> oxalic acid is needed to neutralize <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>mL</mi></math> of sodium hydroxide solution. What is the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>mL</mi></math> of the given sodium hydroxide solution?
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.5</mo><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> oxalic acid is needed to neutralize <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>mL</mi></math> of sodium hydroxide solution. What is the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mtext> </mtext><mi>mL</mi></math> of the given sodium hydroxide solution?
solution:
millimoles of oxalic acid =50×0.5

milli equivalent of oxalic acid =50×0.5×2

millimoles of NaOH=25×M= milli equivalent of NaOH

∴50×0.5×2=25×M

⇒M=2

M=2 so, 1000 ml NaOH solution contains

=2 mol of NaOH

=2×40=80g NaOH

∴ 50ml NaOH solution contains =801000×50=4 g

subject: Chemistry
q: During the reaction of permanganate with thiosulphate, the change in oxidation of manganese occurs by value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>. Identify which of the below medium will favour the reaction.
question: During the reaction of permanganate with thiosulphate, the change in oxidation of manganese occurs by value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>. Identify which of the below medium will favour the reaction.
options: Both aqueous acidic and faintly alkaline
solution:
In neutral or weakly alkaline solution oxidation state of Mn changes by 3 unit. The reaction between permanganate and thiosulfate is a redox reaction, in which permanganate acts as an oxidizing agent and thiosulfate acts as a reducing agent.

MnO4⊝+S2O3-2→MnO2+SO4-2

This ionic mechanism is favoured in neutral aqueous medium.

question:
Identify the incorrect statements regarding primary standard of titrimetric analysis.
(A) It should be purely available in dry form.
(B) It should not undergo chemical change in air.
(C) It should be hygroscopic and should react with another chemical instantaneously and stoichiometrically.
(D) It should be readily soluble in water.
(E) \(\mathrm{KMnO}_4 ~\&~ \mathrm{NaOH}\) can be used as primary standard.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
$\mathrm{KMnO}_4 \& \mathrm{NaOH} \rightarrow$ Secondary standard.
Primary standard should not be Hygroscopic.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I: S8 solid undergoes disproportionation reaction under alkaline conditions to form S2− and S2O32−

Statement II: ClO4− can undergo disproportionation reaction under acidic condition.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I: S8 solid undergoes disproportionation reaction under alkaline conditions to form S2− and S2O32−

Statement II: ClO4− can undergo disproportionation reaction under acidic condition.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct
Both statement I and statement II are incorrect
Both statement I and statement II are correct
solution:
A disproportionation reaction is a reaction in which the same element is simultaneously oxidized and reduced. In this type of reaction, a single substance gives two products, one is oxidized while the other is reduced.

S1:S8+12OH⊝→4S2−+2S2O32−+6H2O

S2:ClO4⊝ cannot undergo disproportionation reaction as chlorine is present in its highest oxidation state.

Hence, statement I is correct and II is incorrect.

question: The strength of an aqueous <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> solution is most accurately determined by titrating: (Note: consider that an appropriate indicator is used)
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">q</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> in a pipette and aqueous oxalic acid in a burette

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">q</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> in a burette and aqueous oxalic acid in a conical flask
Aq.NaOH in a burette and concentrate H2SO4 in a conical flask
Aq.NaOH in a volumetric flask and concentrated H2SO4 in a conical flask
solution:
Oxalic acid is a primary standard solution while H2SO4 is a secondary standard solution.

Titration is a common laboratory method of quantitative chemical analysis to determine the concentration of an identified analyte. A reagent, termed the titrant or titrator, is prepared as a standard solution of known concentration and volume.

The volume of its solution required for the complete neutralization of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>24</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo> </mo><mi>NaOH</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> (Nearest integer)
solution:
millimole of NaOH=0.24×25
For nuetralisation, equal moles of monoacidic base and monobasic acid must be present:

∴ millimole of acid =0.24×25

⇒ mass of acid =0.24×25×24.2 g

for pure acid,

V=wd;(d=1.21 kg/L=1.21 g/ml)

∴V=0.24×25×24.21.12×10-3

=120×10-3ml

=12×10-2ml

subject: Chemistry
q: The oxidation states of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi>P</mi><mo>'</mo></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><mo>,</mo></math> respectively, are :
question: The oxidation states of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi>P</mi><mo>'</mo></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><mo>,</mo></math> respectively, are :
solution:
Oxidation state of P in H4P2O7, H4P2O5 and H4P2O6 is 5, 3 & 4 respectively 

H4P2O7
2x+4(+1)+7(-2)=0
x=+5
H4P2¯O5
2x+4(+1)+5(-2)=0
x=+3
H4P2¯O6
2x+4(+1)+6(-2)=0
x=+4

subject: Chemistry
q:
Total number of species from the following which can undergo disproportionation reaction ___________.
H2O2,ClO3-,P4,Cl2,Ag,Cu+1, F2,NO2, K+
question:
Total number of species from the following which can undergo disproportionation reaction ___________.
H2O2,ClO3-,P4,Cl2,Ag,Cu+1, F2,NO2, K+
solution:
The compounds or ions or elements in which central atom has intermediate oxidation state undergo a disproportionation reaction.

Disproportionation reactions are the reactions in which the same element/compound gets oxidised and reduced simultaneously.

1)Cu+ → Cu2+ + Cu

2) 2H2O-12→H2O-2+O02

3) 3 Cl2 + 6 OH− → 5Cl− + ClO3−+ 3 H2O

 4) 2NO2(g)+2OH−(aq)→NO2−(aq)+H2O(l)+NO3−

5) P4+3OH-+3H2O→PH3+3H2PO2-

Here, the chlorine reactant is in oxidation state 0, and if we look at the products, the chlorine in the Cl− ion has an oxidation number of −1, where it has undergone reduction. Meanwhile, the oxidation number of the chlorine in the ClO3- ion is +5, which means it has been oxidised.

Hence, H2O2, Cu+, Cl2,ClO3,P4,NO2 shows disproportionation reaction.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : An aqueous solution of KOH when for volumetric analysis, its concentration should be checked before the use.

Reason (R) : On aging,KOH solution absorbs atmospheric CO2.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A) : An aqueous solution of KOH when for volumetric analysis, its concentration should be checked before the use.

Reason (R) : On aging,KOH solution absorbs atmospheric CO2.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: (A) is not correct but (R) is correct
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
solution:
KOH absorb CO2 based on the following reaction:
CO2(g) + 2KOH(aq) → K2CO3(aq) + H2O(l)
Thus, concentration of Carbon dioxide decreases over time, prior to volumetric analysis concentration of potassium hydroxide should be checked.

question:

Given : 2Cu2++4I-→Cu2I2+I2I2 + 2S2O3-2  →  2I— + S4O6-2
solution:
neq. of I2=neq of Na2 S2O3=20×0.02×10-3

2×nmol of I2=0.4 ×10-3

nmol of I2=0.2 m mol

nmol of Cu+2=0.2×2×10-3

Cu+2=0.4×10-310×10-3=0.04=4×10-2

The molarity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> solution is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math>
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>02</mn><msub><mi>MK</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>Cr</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>7</mn></msub></math> solution is used for the titration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> solution in the acidic medium. The molarity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> solution is____<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math>
solution:
Equivalents of  K2Cr2O7= Equivalents of Fe2+

⇒(Molarity × volume × n.f) of K2Cr2O7= (molarity × volume × n.f) of Fe2+

n.f=n-factor(valence factor)

⇒0.02×20×6=M×10×1

⇒M=0.24

⇒ Molarity =24×10-2

subject: Chemistry
q: $500$ mL of $0.2$ M MnO$_4^-$ solution in basic medium when mixed with $500$ mL of $1.5$ M KI solution, oxidises iodide ions to liberate molecular iodine. This liberated iodine is then titrated with a standard $x$ M thiosulphate solution in presence of starch till the end point. If $300$ mL of thiosulphate was consumed, then the value of $x$ is __________.
question: $500$ mL of $0.2$ M MnO$_4^-$ solution in basic medium when mixed with $500$ mL of $1.5$ M KI solution, oxidises iodide ions to liberate molecular iodine. This liberated iodine is then titrated with a standard $x$ M thiosulphate solution in presence of starch till the end point. If $300$ mL of thiosulphate was consumed, then the value of $x$ is __________.
solution:
First, we calculate the millimoles of the reactants:
Millimoles of $\text{MnO}_4^-$ = $500 \text{ mL} \times 0.2 \text{ M} = 100 \text{ mmol}$
Millimoles of $\text{I}^-$ = $500 \text{ mL} \times 1.5 \text{ M} = 750 \text{ mmol}$

In a basic medium, $\text{MnO}_4^-$ is reduced to $\text{MnO}_2$. The change in oxidation state of Mn is from $+7$ to $+4$, so its n-factor is $3$.
The problem explicitly states that iodide ions are oxidized to molecular iodine ($\text{I}_2$). The change in oxidation state for iodine is from $-1$ to $0$, so the n-factor for $\text{I}^-$ is $1$.

Equivalents of $\text{MnO}_4^-$ = $100 \times 3 = 300 \text{ meq}$
Equivalents of $\text{I}^-$ = $750 \times 1 = 750 \text{ meq}$

Since $\text{MnO}_4^-$ is the limiting reagent, the equivalents of $\text{I}_2$ produced will be equal to the equivalents of $\text{MnO}_4^-$ consumed.
Equivalents of $\text{I}_2$ formed = $300 \text{ meq}$

The liberated iodine is titrated with thiosulphate ($\text{S}_2\text{O}_3^{2-}$):
$\text{I}_2 + 2\text{S}_2\text{O}_3^{2-} \rightarrow 2\text{I}^- + \text{S}_4\text{O}_6^{2-}$

For thiosulphate, the n-factor is $1$ (as $2$ moles of $\text{S}_2\text{O}_3^{2-}$ lose $2$ moles of electrons).
From the law of equivalence:
Equivalents of $\text{S}_2\text{O}_3^{2-}$ = Equivalents of $\text{I}_2$
$\text{Molarity} \times \text{Volume (in mL)} \times \text{n-factor} = 300$
$x \times 300 \times 1 = 300$
$x = 1$

Answer: $1$

subject: Chemistry
q: X and Y are the number of electrons involved, respectively during the oxidation of $\mathrm{I}^{-}$to $\mathrm{I}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{S}^{2-}$ to S by acidified $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$. The value of $\mathrm{X}+\mathrm{Y}$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
question: X and Y are the number of electrons involved, respectively during the oxidation of $\mathrm{I}^{-}$to $\mathrm{I}_{2}$ and $\mathrm{S}^{2-}$ to S by acidified $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$. The value of $\mathrm{X}+\mathrm{Y}$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
In acidic medium, $K_2Cr_2O_7$ acts as a strong oxidizing agent where $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$ is reduced to $Cr^{3+}$.
The reduction half-reaction is: $Cr_2O_7^{2-} + 14H^+ + 6e^- \rightarrow 2Cr^{3+} + 7H_2O$.
For the oxidation of $I^-$ to $I_2$:
The balanced oxidation half-reaction is $2I^- \rightarrow I_2 + 2e^-$.
To balance the electrons with the reduction half-reaction (6 electrons), we multiply the oxidation half-reaction by 3:
$6I^- \rightarrow 3I_2 + 6e^-$.
Thus, the number of electrons involved in the balanced redox reaction for $I^-$ oxidation is $X = 6$.
For the oxidation of $S^{2-}$ to $S$:
The balanced oxidation half-reaction is $S^{2-} \rightarrow S + 2e^-$.
To balance the electrons with the reduction half-reaction (6 electrons), we multiply the oxidation half-reaction by 3:
$3S^{2-} \rightarrow 3S + 6e^-$.
Thus, the number of electrons involved in the balanced redox reaction for $S^{2-}$ oxidation is $Y = 6$.
The value of $X + Y = 6 + 6 = 12$.

subject: Chemistry
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> oxalic acid is required to neutralise <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution, the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution is_______g.
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi></math> oxalic acid is required to neutralise <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution, the amount of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mL</mi></math> of given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> solution is_______g.
solution:
50ml of 0.5 M oxalic acid is completely neutralised by 25ml of NaOH solution. 

For neutralisation reactions, N1V1=N2V2

Normality of oxalic acid = Molarity×2

⇒Noxalic acid=0.5×2=1N

50×1=25×NNaOH

For sodium hydroxide, molarity is the same as normality.

Molarity of sodium hydroxide = 2M

The number of moles of sodium hydroxide= 50×2×10-3 mol.

Hence, the mass of sodium hydroxide =50×2×10-3×40=4g

q:

EO2/H2Oo=+1.23 V;

EBr2/Br-o=+1.09 V;
EAu3+/Auo=1.4 V


The strongest oxidizing agent is
question:
Given that,

EO2/H2Oo=+1.23 V;
ES2O82-/SO42-o=2.05 V
EBr2/Br-o=+1.09 V;
EAu3+/Auo=1.4 V


The strongest oxidizing agent is
solution:
For strongest oxidizing agent, the standard reduction potential should be highest.

An oxidizing agent, or oxidising agent is a substance that has the ability to oxidize other substances — in other words to accept their electrons. Common oxidizing agents are oxygen, hydrogen peroxide and the halogens.

subject: Chemistry
q: In the reaction of oxalate with permanganate in acidic medium, the number of electrons involved in producing one molecule of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is:
question: In the reaction of oxalate with permanganate in acidic medium, the number of electrons involved in producing one molecule of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>CO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is:
solution:
2 MnO4-+5C2O42-+16H+→10 CO2+2 Mn2++8H2O

C goes from +3 oxidation state to +4 oxidation state.

Total 10 e- are transferred for 10 molecules of CO2.

Hence, for 1 molecule of CO2, only one e- is transferred.
So, number of electrons involved for 1 molecule of CO2  is 1.

q:
250 mL of 0.5M NaOH was added to 500 mL of 1M HCl. The number of unreacted HCl molecules in the solution after the complete reaction is p×1021. Find out p
(Nearest integer) NA=6.022×1023
question:
250 mL of 0.5M NaOH was added to 500 mL of 1M HCl. The number of unreacted HCl molecules in the solution after the complete reaction is p×1021. Find out p
(Nearest integer) NA=6.022×1023
solution:
We known that no. of moles =Vlitre × Molarity & No. of millimoles =Vml× Molarity so millimoles of NaOH=250×0.5
=125
Millimoles of HCl=500×1=500
Now reaction is

so millimoles of HCl left =375 Moles of HCl=375×10-3
No. of HCl molecules =6.022×1023×375×10-3
=225.8×1021
≃226×1021=226

subject: Chemistry
q: 200 cc of $x \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$ potassium dichromate is required to oxidise 750 cc of 0.6 M Mohr's salt solution in acidic medium. Here $x=$ $\_\_\_\_$.
question: 200 cc of $x \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$ potassium dichromate is required to oxidise 750 cc of 0.6 M Mohr's salt solution in acidic medium. Here $x=$ $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
Mohr's salt ($FeSO_4 \cdot (NH_4)_2SO_4 \cdot 6H_2O$) is a source of $Fe^{2+}$ ions.
The redox reaction with potassium dichromate in acidic medium is:
$6Fe^{2+} + Cr_2O_7^{2-} + 14H^+ \to 6Fe^{3+} + 2Cr^{3+} + 7H_2O$.
Moles of $Fe^{2+}$ from Mohr's salt solution:
$n(Fe^{2+}) = 0.6 \text{ M} \times 0.750 \text{ L} = 0.45 \text{ mol}$.
From the stoichiometry, 6 moles of $Fe^{2+}$ require 1 mole of $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$.
Moles of $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$ required:
$n(Cr_2O_7^{2-}) = \frac{0.45}{6} = 0.075 \text{ mol}$.
Molarity of potassium dichromate:
$M = \frac{0.075 \text{ mol}}{0.200 \text{ L}} = 0.375 \text{ M} = 375 \times 10^{-3} \text{ M}$.
Therefore, $x = 375$.

subject: Chemistry
q:
0.1 M solution of KI reacts with excess of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ and $\mathrm{KIO}_3$ solutions. According to equation
$5 \mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}+6 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow 3 \mathrm{I}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

Identify the correct statements :
(A) 200 mL of KI solution reacts with 0.004 mol of $\mathrm{KIO}_3$
(B) 200 mL of KI solution reacts with 0.006 mol of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
(C) 0.5 L of KI solution produced 0.005 mol of $\mathrm{I}_2$
(D) Equivalent weight of $\mathrm{KIO}_3$ is equal to ( $\frac{\text { Molecular weight }}{5}$ )

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
0.1 M solution of KI reacts with excess of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ and $\mathrm{KIO}_3$ solutions. According to equation
$5 \mathrm{I}^{-}+\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}+6 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow 3 \mathrm{I}_2+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

Identify the correct statements :
(A) 200 mL of KI solution reacts with 0.004 mol of $\mathrm{KIO}_3$
(B) 200 mL of KI solution reacts with 0.006 mol of $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$
(C) 0.5 L of KI solution produced 0.005 mol of $\mathrm{I}_2$
(D) Equivalent weight of $\mathrm{KIO}_3$ is equal to ( $\frac{\text { Molecular weight }}{5}$ )

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{KIO}_3}=\frac{\text { Molecular welght }}{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{f}}} \\ & \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{f}}=5 \\ & \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{KIO}_3}=\frac{\text { Molecular welght }}{5}\end{aligned}$
(D) is correct meq of $\mathrm{KI}=0.1 \times 200=20$ meq of $\mathrm{KIO}_3=4 \times 5=20$
$(\mathrm{A})$ is correct

subject: Chemistry
q:
2MnO4-+bI-+cH2O→xI2+yMnO2+zOH-

If the above equation is balanced with integer coefficients, the value of z is ________.
question:
2MnO4-+bI-+cH2O→xI2+yMnO2+zOH-

If the above equation is balanced with integer coefficients, the value of z is ________.
solution:
Reduction Half reaction

2MnO4-→2MnO2

2MnO4-+4H2O+6e-→2MnO2+8OH-

Oxidation Half reaction

2I-→I2+2e-

6I-→3I2+6e-

Adding oxidation half and reduction half, net reaction is

2MnO4-+6I-+4H2O→3I2+2MnO2+8OH-

⇒z=8

⇒Ans 8

Vm1×0.02×6=0.288144×3×1000
Vm1×0.02×6=2×6
Vm1=100ml
